---
title: "`r params$report_title`"
author: "`r params$report_author`"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: false
    code_folding: hide
params:
  # Engine flag: TRUE only when this template is stitched into the
  # combined report (set by the parent). Leave false for normal renders.
  combined_mode: false
  # Master Data Path
  data_path: !r here::here("1_data", "master_micro_survey.csv")

  # Variables
  group_var: "income_tier"
  value_var: "endline_score"

  # Readability guard: keep only the N most frequent groups, lump the rest
  # into "Other" (prevents 20+ facilities from overcrowding the Y-axis)
  max_groups: 8

  # Policy-Facing Labels
  metric_name: "Endline Test Score"

  # --- Report Text (all editable; leave "" to use the smart default) ---
  report_title: "Distribution Landscape: Ridgeline Plot"
  report_author: "Ibraheem Saqib Ellahi"
  chart_title: ""
  chart_subtitle: ""
  x_label: ""
  y_label: ""
  source_note: "Generated via CERP Analytics Template"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = !isTRUE(params$combined_mode),
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  dev.args = list(bg = "#FAF9F6"),
  fig.path = paste0(here::here("4_output", "figures"), "/"),
  fig.width = 9,
  fig.height = 6,
  fig.retina = 3
)
```

```{r cerp-style, echo=FALSE, results='asis', eval=!isTRUE(params$combined_mode)}
cat("<style>", readr::read_file(here::here("2_R", "cerp_style.css")), "</style>")
```

```{r load-and-prep}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggridges)
source(here::here("2_R", "2.5_helpers.R"), local = TRUE)

# Load data (headers and values whitespace-scrubbed) and validate YAML params
raw_data <- cerp_load(params$data_path)
cerp_validate(raw_data, params[grepl("_var$", names(params))])

# ==============================================================================
# DATA SCRUBBING & PROCESSING BLOCK
# ==============================================================================
plot_data <- raw_data %>%
  # 1. Safely drop NAs
  drop_na(all_of(c(params$group_var, params$value_var))) %>%

  # 2. Scrub strings, enforce numeric values
  mutate(
    Group_Raw = str_to_title(str_squish(as.character(.data[[params$group_var]]))),
    Value = cerp_numeric(.data[[params$value_var]], params$value_var)
  ) %>%
  drop_na(Value) %>%

  # 3. Cardinality guard: keep the max_groups most frequent, lump the rest
  mutate(Group_Lumped = cerp_lump(Group_Raw, n = params$max_groups)) %>%

  # 4. Density protection: a curve needs enough observations
  group_by(Group_Lumped) %>%
  filter(n() >= 3) %>%
  ungroup() %>%

  # 5. Wrap long names, order ridges by median so the eye reads a ranking
  mutate(
    Group = str_wrap(as.character(Group_Lumped), width = 18),
    Group = fct_reorder(Group, Value, .fun = median, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

n_lumped <- sum(str_detect(as.character(plot_data$Group), "^Other"))
```


```{r plot-ridgeline, fig.height=max(4.5, 1 + 0.6 * nlevels(plot_data$Group))}
source(here::here("2_R", "2.0_setup_theme.R"), local = TRUE)

# De-emphasize the lumped "Other" ridge; brand color for real groups
ridge_fills <- setNames(
  ifelse(str_detect(levels(plot_data$Group), "^Other"),
         unname(cerp_cols["neutral"]),
         unname(cerp_cols["primary"])),
  levels(plot_data$Group)
)

ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Value, y = Group, fill = Group)) +

  # Overlapping ridges with an internal median line for instant comparison
  geom_density_ridges(
    scale = 1.7,
    color = cerp_cols[["bg"]],
    linewidth = 0.8,
    alpha = 0.9,
    rel_min_height = 0.01,
    quantile_lines = TRUE,
    quantiles = 2,
    vline_color = "#1b3a4b55"
  ) +

  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::comma, expand = c(0.01, 0)) +
  scale_y_discrete(expand = expansion(add = c(0.2, 1.4))) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = ridge_fills) +

  theme_cerp() +

  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold",
                               color = cerp_cols[["text"]]),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "#e5e1d8", linewidth = 0.45)
  ) +

  labs(
    title = if (nzchar(params$chart_title)) params$chart_title else (paste("Population Distribution:", params$metric_name)),
    subtitle = if (nzchar(params$chart_subtitle)) params$chart_subtitle else (paste0( "Each ridge is a group's full distribution; the vertical line marks its median. ", "Ridges are ordered by median, highest at the top", if (n_lumped > 0) paste0(". Smaller groups are combined into ‘Other’") else "", "." )),
    x = if (nzchar(params$x_label)) params$x_label else (params$metric_name),
    y = if (nzchar(params$y_label)) params$y_label else (NULL),
    caption = params$source_note
  )
```
